title: biscutella coronopifolia subsp. lusitanica
intro: Biscutella coronopifolia subsp. lusitanica is a subspecies of the Biscutella coronopifolia plant, a member of the Brassicaceae family, commonly known as the biscutella plant or crow garlic. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant. Biscutella coronopifolia subsp. lusitanica can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
